intended_use_full: BriefCase is a radiological computer aided triage and notification software indicated for use in the analysis of contrast-enhanced chest CTs (but not dedicated CTPA protocol). The device is intended to assist hospital networks and trained radiologists in workflow triage by flagging and communication of suspected positive cases of Incidental Pulmonary Embolism (iPE) pathologies. For the iPE pathology, the software is only intended to be used on single-energy exams. The device is intended to work with GE and Siemens scanners only.
ground_truth: Reviewer assessment of contrast-enhanced chest CT images for presence or absence of iPE
other_metrics: Time-to-notification: 4.7 minutes (95% CI: 4.4-5.1; Median: 5.0, IQR: 2.3); Standard of care time-to-exam-open: 223.3 minutes (95% CI: 125.8-320.7; Median: 70.4, IQR: 217.6); Mean time difference: 220.9 minutes (95% CI: 122.0-319.9)
